evals.prompt: Choose the baseline stack and top-level structure for a small React and Next app that should stay maintainable without a lot of boilerplate.
evals.expected_output: Guidance that establishes Next.js, strict TypeScript, and Yarn as the baseline, keeps the top-level structure small and feature-first, and avoids duplicating lower-level React or Next rules inside the app-level answer.
assertions: The response makes Yarn the preferred package manager for the Node-based app
The response uses Next.js and strict TypeScript as the app baseline
The response distinguishes app-level structure from lower-level React or Next details

evals.prompt: Review whether this requirement needs a full React and Next application or could stay a smaller standalone browser app.
evals.expected_output: A review that compares whole-app infrastructure against a standalone alternative and routes smaller browser-only requirements toward the standalone app skill when appropriate.
assertions: The response explicitly compares a full app against a standalone browser app option
The response routes framework-free cases toward the standalone app skill
The response does not collapse into component-level React advice as the main answer
